what_it_does: An acceptance test file that exercises the runtime session-channels organ, post router, edge fold, and MCP face through five labeled sections (A-E), using isolated temporary FsStore scratch directories and a shared check/expect_teaching harness.
observations: Five sections map to R2.2-R2.5 requirements: A channel lifecycle, B cross-process seq uniqueness, C post routing, D edges, E MCP face.
Uses subprocess.Popen with an inline child code string to hammer the channels leaf from four processes.
Defines _registry_over to mirror Suite.get_agent_session contract without importing Suite in early sections.
expect_teaching helper asserts ValueError refusals contain expected teaching terms.
Per-section temporary directories named agent-chan-A-, etc.
Checks source-code import bans in session_channels.py and mcp_face/tools/channels.py.
Tests direct fan verbs by liveness, conducted coordinator context, channel history joins, and MCP tool registration/annotations/Literal drift.
standouts: Cross-process append storm (4 processes x 25 events) with hard 120s timeout and unique seq verification.
MCP face round-trip including tool registration, read/write annotations, OPS/ACTIONS Literal drift teeth, and full gather->post->promote->describe->history->edges flow.
Post-router tests enforce that broadcasts never wake members and that conducted posts emit exactly one coordinator intent.
conventions: Docstring lists proven claims and exit codes (0 pass, 1 fail, 2 blocked).
check helper increments a global CHECKS counter and prints ok/FAIL.
Tests use session:// prefixed identifiers and channel:///ga-/ch- URI forms.
Each section gets its own scratch store to enforce isolation.
Source-code text checks enforce floor constraints (no subprocess/ui_claude_session/implement imports).
concerns: Teaching-error assertions rely on brittle substring matching against error messages.
Cross-process test embeds a Python code string with sys.path manipulation, sensitive to module layout changes.
Fan-verb assertions in section C/E assume exact ordering/verb sets that may break if routing policy changes.
Source-code import floor checks are fragile to formatting, comments, or renamed imports.
120s hard timeout could mask intermittent lock contention rather than diagnose it.
notes: A heavy integration acceptance test that treats the channel log as the index and verifies both runtime semantics and the MCP tool surface.
questions: What exact API does runtime/session_channels.py expose for create_channel, add_member, post_to_channel, etc.?
How is graph_lock implemented in FsStore to guarantee cross-process seq uniqueness?
What is the default supervisor probe behavior and how does it affect status_source?
What module layout and NodeRegistry discovery path does Suite require?
How are thread IDs minted and what format do chan- threads use?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring claims it proves R2.2-R2.5 cross-session organs by execution, and the sections cover those exact areas.
intention_signals: The file is trying to enforce that channels are append-only store organs with strict lifecycle rules, that post routing respects liveness without supervisor spawning, and that the MCP face is a thin, honestly-annotated wrapper with drift-proof exported action sets.
novelty: Combines a subprocess-based concurrency storm for the channels leaf with source-code-level floor checks and MCP Literal drift teeth in a single acceptance file.
contribution: Provides an end-to-end acceptance harness that pins channel lifecycle semantics, membership rules, post-routing behavior, edge aggregation, and MCP tool contracts; it also contributes reusable patterns for teaching-error testing and cross-process store safety verification.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test suite for session channels that validates lifecycle, routing, edges, and MCP tooling over isolated temporary stores.
intention_for_embedding: To prove by execution that the cross-session channel fabric, post router, edge fold, and MCP face satisfy the R2.2-R2.5 design contracts.

symbols.does: Increments a global check counter and records pass/fail for a named assertion.
symbols.description: The central assertion helper used throughout the file; it prints ok or FAIL, increments CHECKS, and appends the name to FAILURES when the condition is false.

symbols.does: Runs a callable and asserts it raises a ValueError containing expected teaching terms.
symbols.description: Encapsulates the closed-vocabulary refusal pattern: it catches ValueError, checks that every needle appears in the message, and reports failures when the call does not refuse or uses the wrong exception type.

symbols.does: Builds a registry lookup closure over a given FsStore.
symbols.description: Returns a look(sid) function that loads an agent session and raises a teaching ValueError for unknown sessions, mirroring Suite.get_agent_session without importing Suite in the early sections.

symbols.does: Looks up a session by id and raises a teaching ValueError if missing.
symbols.description: The inner closure returned by _registry_over; it delegates to store.load_agent_session and fabricates the registry's own refusal message for unknown sessions.

symbols.does: Tests channel/gathering lifecycle, membership, mode changes, promotion, and reload.
symbols.description: Section A exercises closed vocabularies, create/add/remove/status semantics, conducted coordinator rules, terminal lifecycle gates, provenance stamping, and verifies the fold survives a fresh FsStore reload.

symbols.does: Spawns subprocesses to verify cross-process seq uniqueness on the channels leaf.
symbols.description: Section B launches four Python subprocesses that each append 25 channel events, then asserts all 100 rows parse, counts are exact, and every seq value is unique across processes.

symbols.does: Tests the post router for direct fan, conducted coordinator intent, statuses, and history.
symbols.description: Section C verifies direct fan verbs by liveness, one shared pre-minted thread, conducted single-intent routing with coordinator context, declared-plus-registry status projection, and channel history joined to thread traffic.

symbols.does: Tests the edge fold after real mail and channel fan traffic.
symbols.description: Section D seeds agent mail between sessions, posts through a channel, and checks that edges_for returns direction-aware counts, verbs, shared threads, followup handles, channel attribution, and honest addressable:false for label peers.

symbols.does: Tests the MCP face registration, annotations, drift teeth, and full round-trip.
symbols.description: Section E imports the MCP tool module, registers tools on a dev FastMCP with a real Suite, checks read/write annotations, compares exported OPS/ACTIONS to Literal signatures, and runs gather->post->promote->add->status->mode->describe->post->history->list->edges.

symbols.does: Runs sections A-E on separate scratch stores and prints the final pass/fail result.
symbols.description: The entry point that creates a temporary directory per section, invokes each test function, tallies CHECKS and FAILURES, and exits 0 for pass, 1 for fail, or 2 if a subprocess storm hung.
